summary: Lead CI fix after systems R2 ACCEPT: pnpm check exposed that adding M6 diplomacy commands directly to exported GameCommandV1 forced forbidden client-core M5 helpers to handle M6 command kinds. The fix keeps client-facing GameCommandV1 as the pre-M6 command union and introduces AuthoritativeGameCommandV1/M6GameCommandV1 for simulation submit/preview, parseGameCommandV1, sim-core, save-format, M6 AI, and M6 tests. No forbidden client-core files were modified.

tests_run.result: PASS; forbidden client-core no longer needs code changes for M6 command kinds.
PASS with authoritative command-tail/save command typing.
PASS with M6 AI authoritative command type.
PASS: format:check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundaries all passed.
PASS: Node and worker-compatible hashes matched for hello, command/query, save/load, M4 replay, and M5 playable loop.
PASS: architecture, determinism, root Vitest 37 files / 259 tests, web Vitest 1 file / 4 tests.
VALID before R3 route update; final validation is run after routing files are written.

decisions: REQUEST R3 REVIEW: AuthoritativeGameCommandV1 is the simulation submit/preview/parser command union and includes M6 diplomacy/legitimacy commands.
Client-facing legacy GameCommandV1 intentionally remains pre-M6 so existing M5 client helpers are not forced to handle M6 commands before M6 client surfaces are authorized.
SimulationMessageV1 preview-command and submit-command payloads use AuthoritativeGameCommandV1, preserving the same command path for player and AI M6 actions.
sim-core, save-format, M6 AI, and M6 tests use AuthoritativeGameCommandV1 through local aliases; no forbidden client-core changes were made.
This is an architecture-sensitive boundary fix and must receive systems_architect ACCEPT before PR integration proceeds.
risks: The public protocol now exposes both GameCommandV1 and AuthoritativeGameCommandV1; reviewers must confirm this does not create confusing API drift for M6 downstream tasks.
Future M6 client surfaces should either consume authoritative commands explicitly or introduce narrower client command/read-model types under their own task scope.
Untracked ui-samples/ remains unrelated and untouched.
requested_action: Review the R3 CI/API-boundary fix at commit a379d188c10fc93f19405a533e03b2e52f61a1fc. Accept only if the type boundary preserves M6 command parity without hidden privileges and without requiring forbidden client-core changes for this task.
